mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >70% of instances following the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC over rolling 3-year windows or yields negative returns in >60% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate margins and generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations across rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, immediately depressing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns follow inflation spikes in >55% of cases, negating the tightening premium effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of triggered periods or shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with increased corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict return direction with a rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an imminent supply-side contraction and inventory buildup, triggering rapid short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of periods following the trigger, indicating the signal is overwhelmed by liquidity or policy support.
